The guy got into my space bubble. And by the way, he chased down my manager, the tour promoter, everybody and kept going in their face. Everyone was like, what is wrong with this guy, Boris? Now understand this, okay? That bubble of two and a half to three feet, when you invade it, people will not listen to what you have to say. They will literally back off and you're doing this almost chasing dance around. All right? So you got to be really aware of this. These close standards out there, if that's you, you just got to be aware and stop it. Stop. You can't get into rapport people when you're that close. I'm telling you, it's just toxic. And I said in the intro to this module, death by a thousand cuts. Mistake, mistake, mistake. Before you know, you're bleeding out and it's all before it started. You get it? The key here is in the Western world, remember two and a half to three feet. And when you're in Asia, it's closer. That's why suppose you go to Asia, you feel weird, but to them it's normal, but only by six inches. That's an important six inches. They might be two feet apart there. One point I didn't raise in the training was the formal bow, okay? Because bows are a very special thing in the sense that they differ from country to country. How you bow in Japan is different than how you bow in China, how you bow in Korea. One point, all right, I'm going to include this, there's some articles and links in the resource centers. It's a very specialized thing here, right? And I'm not expert, I admit. But here's one thing I do know. What you never want to do is both bow and shake hands. One or the other. And the bottom line is unless you're in the most formal of settings, almost every single person of substance in that part of the world will accept a handshake, a cooperated handshake and be fine with it. Unless you're going to some sort of like, you know, royalty or old line emperor sort of stuff, right? You get it? So a handshake will get you through. But what you don't do is do the handshake and that formal bow. That's a big mistake, right? It's a handshake, hand your collar to hands. That's their custom over there, okay? One more point. Remember, there are fundamental differences here between how a man approaches a man and a man approaches a woman, cornering off if you're a man, you don't want to be directly in front of women, head on, you man to woman, hands above waist level. And in the end, it's basic stuff. The key is, is you have to apply it actively, consciously. In the beginning, whatever patterns you've laid down that are incorrect, the only way to break those patterns is to consciously, for the next few days, the next, when the weeks that come after, consciously apply what I'm teaching you. I promise it won't take long to rewiring your brain to do this stuff, right? I've been saying it every day this week, this is the easiest stuff for you to master. Body language is not difficult.
